title: CAPECO MONEY MANAGEMENT - 211info
text: MAIN OFFICE CAPECO MONEY MANAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides Representative Payee services. 211 SE Byers Avenue Pendleton, OR 97801 Eligibility: -Oregon Money Management Program (OMMP): Age 18 and older and low-income -CAPECO Money Management Program (CMMP): Age 59 and older and adults with disabilities Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm, Friday 8am-noon Email (541) 276-1926 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 276-1926 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Must be referred, call or visit website for more information FEES: Varies by service DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: -CMMP: Baker, Gilliam, Grant, Harney, Hood River, Malheur, Morrow, Sherman, Umatilla, Union, Wallowa, and Wasco counties and Cities of College Place, Dayton, Waitsburg, and Walla Walla -OMMP: Baker, Gilliam, Grant, Harney, Hood River, Malheur, Morrow, Sherman, Umatilla, Union, Wallowa, and Wasco counties TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Enter from the front lobby doors on SE Byers Avenue, south side of the building. Services: Representative Payee Services Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - AREA AGENCY ON AGING COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - COMMUNITY ACTION AGENCY FOOD - HOME DELIVERED MEALS HEALTH SUPPORTIVE SERVICES - AGING AND DISABILITY RESOURCE CONNECTION HOUSING/SHELTER - HOMEOWNERSHIP PROGRAM HOUSING/SHELTER - WEATHERIZATION SERVICES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OPTIONS COUNSELING INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON PROJECT INDEPENDENCE – MEDICAID INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SUPPORTIVE SERVICES FOR VETERAN FAMILIES INFORMATION SERVICES - FAMILY CAREGIVER SUPPORT PROGRAM MATERIAL GOODS - HELPING HAND UTILITIES - UTILITY ASSISTANCE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
